name: Corvin Castle
description: This fairy-tale castle would make Dracula (who is said to have been imprisoned there) jealous.

name: Hoia-Baciu Forest
description: Some call this eerie forest of crooked trees the "Bermuda Triangle of Romania."

name: Salina Turda
description: This massive underground salt mine now holds a sci-fi theme park in its depths.

name: Bigar Waterfall
description: Moss and stone turn this waterfall into an otherworldly liquid veil.

name: Bran Castle
description: The possible imprisonment of Vlad the Impaler here earned the medieval castle its nickname, "Dracula's Castle."

name: Peleș Castle
description: This elaborately decorated, fairytale-like Romanian royal palace is one of the most striking castles in Europe.

name: Cărturești Carusel Bookstore
description: This once-derelict building is now one of the most beautiful bookstores in the world.

name: Snagov Monastery
description: This solitary monastery may hold the remains of Vlad the Impaler (but probably not).

name: Poenari Castle
description: The legendary cliffside castle of three-time Voivode of Wallachia, Vlad the Impaler.

name: The Merry Cemetery
description: A countryside Romanian cemetery filled with colorful crosses bearing lyrical, ironic, or satirical rhymes.

name: Tunnel of Love
description: The romantic botanic tunnel feels like a well-kept secret.

name: Decebal's Head
description: A giant portrait of an ancient king looms over the river.

name: Constanța Casino
description: Once an opulent Art Nouveau hotspot, the Constanța Casino is one of Romania's most beautiful abandoned buildings.

name: Curtea Veche (Old Princely Court)
description: The bust of Romania’s most notorious ruler, Vlad Dracula aka "Vlad the Impaler," keeps watch over the ruins of his medieval court.

name: The Sinca Veche Temple Cave
description: No one knows who built this strange cave of worship or exactly why.

name: Relics of Zlătari Church
description: The preserved arm of a third-century sorcerer is believed to have the power to lift curses and spells.

name: The Mountain Sphinx
description: Conspiracy theories and occult legends surround this mysterious natural rock formation.

name: Râpa Roșie
description: Romania's "Red Ravine" is a little-known natural wonder that bears a resemblance to the Grand Canyon.

name: Geamana, Romania
description: Even drowned in an ocean of toxic waste, the remnants of this Romanian village stand tall.

name: Berca Mud Volcanoes
description: A muddy alien landscape that is home to rare species of exotic plants.

name: Palace of the Parliament
description: This communist behemoth is the heaviest building in the world and a legacy of a brutal regime.

name: Densuș Church
description: One of the oldest churches in Romania is believed to have been built on the site of a pagan temple.

name: Zoological Museum
description: The Natural History Museum that time forgot.

name: Strada Sforii (Rope Street)
description: This snug medieval lane is one of the narrowest streets in the world.

name: Museum of Pharmacy
description: Known as the Hintz House, this medieval pharmacy has been an apothecary's haven for nearly 450 years.

name: Biserica Neagră (The Black Church)
description: A fire once nearly devoured this gorgeous 14th-century Gothic cathedral.

name: Doftana Penitentiary
description: This enormous abandoned prison was once dubbed the "Bastille of Romania."

name: Sibiu's Houses with Eyes
description: Something's watching you.

name: Bears' Cave
description: This bone-filled cave got its name after they found 140 bear skeletons inside.

name: Lacul Sfanta Ana
description: Romania's only crater lake with water as pure as if distilled.

name: Transylvania's "Hollywood" Signs
description: The European towns of Brașov and Râșnov each have proud signs reminiscent of the Hollywood sign.

name: Museum of the Communist Consumer
description: A collection of items from a way of life that no longer exists.

name: Towers of Sighișoara
description: Nine centuries-old fortresses guard the walls of this medieval Romanian city.

name: Salina Praid
description: Gigantic Romanian salt mines used to heal respiratory conditions.

name: Arcul de Triumf
description: Romania's triumphal arch looks out over Bucharest.

name: Pharmacy Museum of Sibiu
description: A stunning 18th century pharmacy and medieval chemist's collection.

name: Dimitrie Leonida Technical Museum
description: Check out engineering marvels of the past at this unintentionally retrofuturist museum.

name: Viscri Fortified Church
description: A medieval church built up to defend its village.

name: One Wood Monastery
description: A Romanian church built from one single oak.

name: Sighișoara Saxon Cemetery
description: This hauntingly beautiful cemetery outside a medieval citadel is full of winding paths and ivy-covered graves.

name: Muzeul Micul Paris (Little Paris Museum)
description: This museum offers a journey back to the Belle Epoque era of Romania.

name: Cavnic Gravity Hill
description: A spooky spot along the road where things mysteriously seem to move uphill by themselves.

name: Sarmizegetusa Regia
description: Once the legendary capital of the Dacian Kingdom, this city has been reduced to mere ruins.

name: Chiajna Monastery
description: Locals believe this abandoned ruin on the outskirts of Bucharest is haunted.

name: Iulia Hasdeu Castle
description: This eccentric folly house was designed by Romanian thinker Bogdan-Petriceicu Hasdeu, with a helping hand from his deceased daughter's spirit.

name: Cârța Monastery
description: Climbing to the top of the cathedral's bell tower offers sweeping views of these medieval ruins.

name: Abandoned Baths of Băile Herculane
description: An old spa town in the mountains of Romania where, according to legend, the great hero Hercules once bathed.

name: The "August von Spiess" Museum of Hunting
description: Packed to the ceiling with antique taxidermy and hunting arms.

name: Trovants of Costesti, Romania
description: The growing stones of Romania.

name: The House of Ceaușescu
description: The kitsch mansion of the former Romanian president Nicolae Ceaușescu features a golden bathroom.

name: Memorial of Rebirth
description: This memorial to the victims of the Romanian Revolution of 1989 is often compared to a potato on a skewer.

name: Round Church of Geoagiu
description: This 11th-century structure is one of the oldest churches in Romania.

name: Prometheus Statue at Vidraru Dam
description: The ancient Greek Titan guards this massive dam holding a lightning bolt to symbolize electricity.

name: St. Michael’s Church
description: This Transylvanian church has changed its denomination four times.

name: Memorial of the Victims of Communism and of the Resistance
description: A memorial museum to the political prisoners who were held at Sighet Prison, once one of the most feared places in Romania.

name: Mogoșoaia Palace
description: 17th century palace once a meeting place for politicians.

name: Castle of Magyarózd
description: Home of the first female Hungarian poet, where her secret writing lay hidden for over a century.

name: Politehnica Metro Station
description: This metro station doubles as an unintentional paleontology museum.

name: Văcărești Natural Park
description: After sitting unused for decades, this abandoned communist infrastructure became a wildlife utopia.

name: Horezu Monastery
description: Religious structure known for its sculpted details.

name: Suceviţa Monastery
description: This fortress-like monastery is covered with intricate frescoes.

name: Caru' cu Bere
description: Historic beer hall in Bucharest with magnificent stained glass and a lively folk band.

name: Chimopar
description: A chemical factory damaged by two earthquakes and an explosion that became abandoned after bankruptcy.

name: Curtea de Argeș Monastery
description: According to a gruesome legend, an architect sacrificed his wife to build this spectacular, 16th-century cathedral.

name: Solacoglu Inn
description: An abandoned pasta factory in the centre of Bucharest, once home to Bulgarian revolutionaries and a hub of crime.

name: Cascada Cailor (Horses Waterfall)
description: A stunning waterfall in the mountains of Romania.

name: Fortress of Deva
description: Visit an imposing 13th-century Transylvanian citadel lurking not far from the region's more heavily touristed attractions.

name: Bârsana Monastery
description: The most impressive example of contemporary Romanian architecture in wood

name: Communist Film Depot
description: Several rooms flooded with 35mm film reel.

name: Casa Vlad Dracul
description: This is the birthplace of Vlad the Impaler Vlad III Dracul.

name: Glykon
description: An ancient Roman snake god, carved expertly into stone.

name: Monument to the Heroes of the Air
description: This sculpture used famed boxer Joe Louis as the inspiration behind its body.

name: Ancient City of Histria
description: Once a thriving Greek port, now a windswept ruin lost to time.

name: Museum of Living in Communism
description: A habitable museum, or a time machine into Romania’s Communist era?

name: The Bridge of Lies
description: Legendary pedestrian bridge in the centre of Romanian city Sibiu.

name: Museum of Romanian Records
description: Unique museum with a dizzying array of collectibles, from corkscrews to typewriters and much more.

name: Vaser Valley Forestry Railway
description: The last active narrow-gauge forest railway in Europe.

name: Roma Mansions of Buzescu
description: Roma who struck it rich in the 1980s built extravagant mansions in this small Romanian town.

name: Palatul Administrativ in Satu Mare
description: This Brutalist building was once the tallest in Romania.

name: Romanian Kitsch Museum
description: A celebration of all things tacky, garish, and bizarrely creative in the Romanian capital.

name: Flooded Village of Bezidu Nou
description: This Romanian village may have been drowned, but it is long from forgotten.

name: Trajan and Wolf Statue
description: One of the most controversial statues in Romania is an emperor with no clothes.
